column1: Hu Tao is a powerful Pyro Polearm user in Genshin Impact who relies on Vaping her Charged Attacks to deal high on-field single-target damage. This Guide will walk you through Hu Tao’s Talent priority
column2: best weapons

column1: Doesn’t rely on Bennett or any other specific units aside from Xingqiu/Yelan.
Rewards players who invest time into practicing her combos.
Strong speedrunning unit at all investment levels.
Mechanically difficult and has either notable positioning issues or a severe drop in DPS without proper Charged Attack cancels.
Limited AoE damage outside of Burst nuke setups.

column2: survivability
column3: and some playability issues at C0.

column1: Extremely reliant on Xingqiu and/or Yelan
column2: who are both highly in-demand units.

column1: Should I go for Staff of Homa or C1?

C0 with R1 Homa has a similar damage ceiling to C1 with R5 Dragon’s Bane or R5 Deathmatch. C1 with R5 Ballad of the Fjords is slightly better damage on average than C0 with R1 Homa. It’s up to you which option you prefer.
If you specifically want to play Hu Tao
column2: C1 is generally the better option as it makes her more comfortable to play. C1 combos average up about a 20% damage increase over C0 combos of equivalent difficulty. Conversely
column4: R1 Deathmatch or R1 Ballad of the Fjords to R1 Homa can be around a 20-25% damage increase
column5: though the upgrade is smaller if you start from higher-refined or better weapons.

column1: It can take around 210 wishes to obtain a 5* banner weapon
column2: as opposed to the approximate 160 wishes it can take to obtain C1. This makes C1 more cost-effective
column3: especially since even F2P players can eventually obtain a high-refined Dragon’s Bane.

column2: Homa is more versatile for players that will use the weapon on other characters. It is also a guaranteed increase to Hu Tao’s damage floor even if a player is not mechanically skilled enough to use C1’s potential. If you aren’t sure whether you’ll commit to mastering Hu Tao’s combos
column3: Homa can be a safer investment.

column1: Is Hu Tao bad without C1?
Hu Tao is a good unit at C0. However
column2: C1 improves not only her overall DPS potential but also her ease of use by making her cancels more fluid
column3: giving her more i-frames by letting her dash cancel
column4: and increasing the total number of Charged Attacks you can execute. Her damage at C0 is still more than enough for most content
column5: but the step up to C1 is noticeable.

column1: Is Xingqiu or Yelan better for Hu Tao?
Xingqiu and Yelan are best when paired together
column2: but Xingqiu is better if you can only bring one. Commonly referred to as Double Hydro
column3: the two greatly complement one another
column4: activate Hydro Resonance to buff Hu Tao and Yelan’s damage
column5: and give Hu Tao extremely consistent Vapes. Xingqiu provides Hu Tao with defensive utility
column6: while Yelan provides a strong ramping DMG% buff.

column1: Pre-C2 Yelan as the sole Hydro unit usually does not apply enough Hydro for Hu Tao to reliably Vape without either precise inputs or external help to maintain the Hydro aura. The latter can come in the form of Fischl or a Hydro-absorbed Kazuha Burst. Note that highly skilled players are capable of vaping all of Hu Tao’s CAs with Yelan to varying degrees of consistency
column2: but reliability of Hydro application is why Xingqiu is generally the more preferred pairing for Hu Tao as a solo Hydro unit.
with Constellations
column3: Yelan has a much higher personal damage ceiling compared to Xingqiu and can provide an HP% buff for Hu Tao via her C4. Additionally
column4: Yelan allows for 18s rotations compared to Xingqiu’s 21-22s rotations. In most Hu Tao teams
column5: the other two teammates also typically have cooldowns shorter than 21s. All else being equal
column6: shortening rotations from 21s to 18s represents a 16.7% DPS increase.

column1: Can I still play Hu Tao if I don’t want to learn her Charged Attack cancels?

column2: Hu Tao can still perform well with uncancelled Charged Attacks
column3: although well-performed cancels are necessary if you want to reach Hu Tao’s damage ceiling.

column1: Playing C0 Hu Tao without cancels and just spamming her CAs allows you to reach close to the same level of damage as playing her with well-performed jump cancels. Uncancelled N1Cs are about 90% as effective as good N1CJs
column2: while uncancelled N2Cs are around 95% as effective as good N2CJs.
uncancelled CAs have a tendency to make Hu Tao dash far past enemies not large enough to block her. This can cause her to miss subsequent NAs and CAs against such enemies
column3: and makes it harder to focus on one specific enemy in multi-target scenarios. On the other hand
column4: depending on the content you’re facing
column5: it can also help by increasing the amount of opponents hit.

column2: uncancelled Charged Attacks can cause Yelan’s Burst waves to miss
column3: which significantly decreases her damage output. Thus
column4: doing uncancelled CAs is suboptimal if using Yelan.

column1: As shown in the chart in the Combo Comparison section
column2: going from 6N2C to 8N2C can yield a 26% increase in damage. This is on par with the approximate 28% increase you get upgrading from R5 Missive Windspear to R1 Homa
column3: and demonstrates the significant damage increase you can get from improving your mechanical skill on Hu Tao.

column2: Hu Tao’s C1 is essentially useless if you do not perform dash cancels (aside from the comfort that comes with its Stamina reduction). Forgoing dash cancels means passing up C1’s potential ~20% DPS increase when going from C0 to C1 combos of similar difficulty.
Hu Tao can absolutely be played with low mechanical skill
column3: but her best teams (Double Hydro) and optimal combos (especially at C1) yield massive DPS increases with proper cancels.
